skill.name: webapp-testing
skill.description: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.
skill.summary: Automated web application testing toolkit using Playwright for frontend validation and UI debugging.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 38 patterns, but manual review confirms all HIGH-severity findings are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are scanner errors on argparse code. Shell backtick findings are Markdown documentation formatting. Subprocess usage in with_server.py is intentional server management functionality requiring explicit CLI invocation. Hardcoded localhost URLs and temp file writes are expected for local testing toolkit.

low_findings.title: Subprocess with shell=True
low_findings.description: with_server.py uses subprocess.Popen with shell=True to start development servers. This is intentional functionality for a server management helper script. Commands are passed via CLI arguments, requiring explicit user invocation. Risk is mitigated by design - users explicitly provide the server commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of shell=True with user-provided commands, but this is the intended purpose of a server wrapper utility requiring explicit CLI invocation.

content.user_title: Test Web Applications with Playwright Automation
content.value_statement: Local web application testing requires reliable browser automation without manual setup. This toolkit provides Playwright scripts and server management utilities to streamline frontend testing workflows.
actual_capabilities: Launch headless Chromium browsers for automated testing
Capture browser console logs during page interactions
Discover page elements including buttons, links, and form inputs
Take full-page screenshots for visual verification
Manage development server lifecycle with automatic startup and cleanup
Wait for network idle state before executing test actions
limitations: Only supports testing local web applications, not external URLs
Requires Python 3 with Playwright installed in the environment
Server management script supports only HTTP-based readiness checks on ports
Cannot test applications requiring authentication without custom credential handling

use_cases.title: Frontend Regression Testing
use_cases.description: Automate verification of UI components after code changes to catch breaking changes before deployment.

use_cases.title: Development Server Validation
use_cases.description: Automatically start backend and frontend servers, run integration tests, then clean up resources.
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developers running local integration tests

use_cases.title: Console Log Debugging
use_cases.description: Capture and analyze browser console output to identify JavaScript errors and warnings during user flows.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Navigate to my local React app at localhost:3000, wait for the page to fully load, and take a screenshot of the homepage.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with basic browser automation

prompt_templates.title: Element Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Visit my web application and list all buttons, links, and form inputs on the current page with their text content and attributes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Exploring page structure before writing test scripts

prompt_templates.title: Console Log Capture
prompt_templates.prompt: Run my automation script while capturing all browser console messages. Save the logs to a file and report any errors or warnings found.
prompt_templates.scenario: Debugging JavaScript issues during user interactions

prompt_templates.title: Full Integration Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Start my backend server on port 8000 and frontend on port 5173, then run my Playwright test script that logs in, navigates to the dashboard, and verifies key elements are present. Clean up servers after tests complete.
prompt_templates.scenario: Running end-to-end tests with server management

output_examples.input: Discover all interactive elements on localhost:5173
Capture console logs during user flow
output: Console: [error] Failed to load resource: net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED
Captured 15 console messages. Logs saved to outputs/console.log

best_practices: Always wait for networkidle state before interacting with dynamic pages to ensure JavaScript has fully executed
Use the with_server.py helper script to manage server lifecycle instead of manually starting and stopping servers
Run scripts with --help flag first to understand available options before customizing the automation logic
anti_patterns: Do not inspect the DOM or take screenshots before waiting for networkidle on dynamic applications
Avoid reading script source code into context window - use --help documentation and invoke as black-box tools
Do not use fixed timeouts like wait_for_timeout when specific selectors can be waited for with wait_for_selector

faq.question: How do I test an application that requires authentication?
faq.answer: Include login steps in your Playwright script before navigating to protected pages. You can store credentials in environment variables or use Playwright's storage state feature to reuse authenticated sessions.

faq.question: Can I test multiple servers simultaneously?
faq.answer: Yes, with_server.py supports multiple --server and --port pairs. Each server starts in sequence, waits for readiness, then runs your test command after all servers are ready.

faq.question: Why are my tests failing when the page has dynamic content?
faq.answer: Ensure you call page.wait_for_load_state('networkidle') after navigation. For specific elements, use page.wait_for_selector() to wait for elements to appear before interacting.

faq.question: How do I capture browser screenshots for visual debugging?
faq.answer: Use page.screenshot(path='filename.png', full_page=True) to capture the entire page. Screenshots are saved to the specified path and can be viewed after test execution.

faq.question: What browsers are supported for testing?
faq.answer: Playwright supports Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit. This skill defaults to Chromium in headless mode for reliability. You can modify scripts to use other browsers if needed.

faq.question: How do I install the required dependencies?
faq.answer: Install Playwright with pip install playwright, then run playwright install to download browser binaries. Ensure Python 3.7 or higher is available in your environment.
